harmony_preparation: Context-dependent: consonant inside upper harmony, but 4th above bass is treated like a 4-3 suspension or 6-4 sonority and normally prepared.
harmony_resolution: If dissonant above bass, resolve 4-3 down by step or 6-4 to 5-3/cadential resolution.
harmony_consecutive_permissibility: Bass-upper consecutive 4ths forbidden except noted exception; upper-voice 4ths not prohibited.
harmony_consecutive_quantity_max: bass-upper 1; upper voices no source max
counterpoint_preparation: In strict 2-part counterpoint treat as dissonance: prepare by tie for 4-3 suspension or use as passing by step.
counterpoint_resolution: Resolve down by step to 3rd if suspension; passing form continues by step to consonance.
counterpoint_consecutive_permissibility: No structural consecutive 4ths in strict 2-part work.

harmony_consecutive_permissibility: Context-only; no general rule allowing same 13ths as parallels.
counterpoint_preparation: Compound major 6th: consonance in counterpoint; no preparation.
counterpoint_resolution: No required resolution; use smooth melodic motion.
counterpoint_consecutive_permissibility: Yes; several consecutive compound sixths allowed but avoid monotony.
